level_signals: 5+ years recent experience as a Technical Program Manager

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's Degree with experience in Computer Science, Software Engineering or other related discipline.
5+ years recent experience as a Technical Program Manager at a technology company.
Experience in large-scale distributed systems and system architecture.
Experience working with platform engineers and directly engaging on product development, with a thorough understanding of software development processes.
Project planning and execution skills that include reviewing technical design documents, estimating, and scheduling.

You're experienced in working with platform and/or product engineers and large-scale distributed systems. You can speak to system architecture, scalability and reliability decisions, and design trade offs.
You're a strong leader with the ability to collaborate and build relationships across organizations, bridge gaps between teams, and successfully influence without authority.

You have excellent written & verbal communication skills with the ability to communicate with confidence, clarity, and focus. You can convey concepts and considerations to non-experts and experts alike.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: driving important engineering-wide initiatives related to convenience & grocery and empowering local economies by helping merchants open, grow and operate more profitably.
Platform areas span mobile and backend interfaces.

scope.surface: Dasher & Logistics, Marketplace, Merchant, and New Verticals Engineering platforms (mobile and backend interfaces)
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform adoption by product engineering teams
core_responsibilities: Own Platform Programs partnering with engineering organizations to establish vision, strategy, technical direction and success metrics
Drive optimizations related to engineering OKRs planning and develop frameworks for team execution/tracking
Develop full understanding of technical stack and interdependencies, contribute to designs
Manage all stakeholder expectations from leadership to individual teams
Manage timely communications to leadership and across teams to ensure accountability
Develop processes to streamline teamwork and optimize velocity

green_flags: Compensation ranges disclosed across three levels (I4–I6), providing transparency on seniority banding
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail (401k, parental leave, healthcare, etc.)
Clear scope: four named engineering org areas with specific focus areas
Role reports into a centralized TPM org, suggesting career structure and peer community
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous — NYC notice suggests some remote eligibility but no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated for the role
Three leveling bands (I4, I5, I6) are listed without clarity on which level this posting targets, creating uncertainty about seniority expectations

JD lists multiple engineering orgs (Dasher & Logistics, Marketplace, Merchant, New Verticals) without specifying which team the candidate would join, which could indicate a pooled/undifferentiated hiring process
